name: Rieguabaxu, Naviana, Faro de Cabo Vidio – ruta por la costa
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cudillero offers diverse terrain, blending mountainous geography with a rugged coastline. The region features brañas and mountains that descend towards valleys and a rocky coast, providing varied experiences for riders. High cliffs, averaging 80 meters, dominate the coastline, particularly at Cabo Vidio, forming part of the Protected Landscape of the Western Coast. Inland, riders can explore lush green hills, verdant valleys, and rivers. Best mountain bike trails around Cudillero The most popular mountain bike trail is Rieguabaxu, Naviana, Faro de Cabo Vidio – ruta por la costa, a 21.7 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabo Vidio and Northern Route MTB Loop, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This trail features dramatic coastal views and challenging sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Busto Lighthouse loop from Soto de Luiña, a 42.5 miles (68.3 km) trail leading through coastal viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Cudillero is defined by mountainous terrain, rugged coastlines, and lush green hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal paths to more challenging inland climbs. The routes in Cudillero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cudillero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cudillero?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Cudillero, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cudillero?
A: Cudillero's mountain bike trails feature a varied topography, blending mountainous geography with a rugged coastline. You'll encounter 'brañas' (high pastures) and mountains that descend towards valleys, as well as a rocky coast with high cliffs. Inland, there are lush green hills and verdant valleys.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails in Cudillero?
A: Yes, Cudillero offers challenging mountain bike trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24615889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Busto Lighthouse loop from Soto de Luiña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 68.3 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10504300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Cendán – Hermitage of San Martín loop from Soto de Luiña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.7 km long and also features over 1000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cudillero?
A: Yes, among the 27 available routes, there are 3 easy mountain bike trails suitable for less experienced riders or families. These routes offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer breathtaking views of the dramatic coastline, including high cliffs and beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silencio beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Aguilar&lt;/a&gt;. You can also pass by significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidio Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regalina Chapel&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, you might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and lush green hills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cudillero?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Cudillero are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027488171/cabo-vidio-and-northern-route-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Vidio and Northern Route MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 30.1 km circular path, offering dramatic coastal views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24615889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Busto Lighthouse loop from Soto de Luiña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cudillero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 25 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging inland climbs to scenic coastal paths, and the dramatic views of the Cantabrian Sea and rugged cliffs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cudillero?
A: Cudillero's climate is influenced by the Cantabrian Sea, offering mild temperatures. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's lush landscapes suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for comfortable riding conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential winter rain.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Cudillero?
A: Yes, Cudillero is known for its panoramic viewpoints. Trails often lead to elevated spots like Mirador de la Garita, Mirador del Pico, and Mirador Cimadevilla, which offer stunning views of the village, harbor, and the expansive Cantabrian Sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24615889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte del Cabo Viewpoint – Busto Lighthouse loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights coastal viewpoints.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Cudillero?
A: While the guide doesn't explicitly state dog-friendly policies for specific trails, many natural areas in Asturias are generally welcoming to dogs on leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at trailheads, especially within protected landscapes like the Western Coast, to ensure compliance.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Cudillero?
A: Cudillero is a well-known destination, and public transport options like ALSA buses and FEVE trains serve the Asturias region. However, specific information on transporting bikes on these services or direct access to trailheads via public transport is not detailed here. It's recommended to check the respective transport providers' policies regarding bicycle carriage.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Cudillero or Asturias?
A: The provided information does not indicate any specific permits required for general mountain biking on trails around Cudillero. However, if you plan to ride within designated natural parks or protected areas, it's always good practice to check for any specific regulations or permit requirements from local authorities to ensure responsible riding.
tips: Inaugurated in 1950, it is located in a walled area, with two independent buildings, on the edge of impressive cliffs almost 100 meters high.

Very nice rocky beach. Plenty of parking space nearby. At the west side of the beach you will find a nice restaurant. There are some walking ways that allow easy access to the whole length of the beach. There is also an "inland beach", resulting from a river that flows into the sea.

The very beautiful church of Santa María de Soto de Luiña, is a construction from the 17th century, and located in the municipality of Cudillero. It forms an old pilgrim hospital (linked to the Camino de Santiago), together with the Rectoral House.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Soto de Luiña
